643.reason: [This lookahead logic is flawed because it assumes that any declaration must not be followed by '(' two tokens ahead, but a VarDecl without initialization (e.g., "int a;") followed by a function definition could break this assumption. Also, if a semicolon is missing (error type i), the parser may incorrectly treat a function as a declaration.]

648.reason: [This lookahead assumes that all functions before main will have their identifier immediately followed by MAINTK in the next token, which is incorrect. It should check for FuncType (VOIDTK/INTTK/CHARTK) followed by IDENFR and then LPARENT to properly identify FuncDef.]
